name: AI-based human-wildlife conflict monitoring
description: Use of computational methods -- such as news article mining, aerial/satellite imagery analysis, and machine learning -- to detect, map, and predict zones of conflict between human communities and wildlife. This domain spans technical pipeline design, ground-truthing against local realities, and evaluation of whether detected hotspots translate into actionable conservation or safety interventions.

name: Digitizing indigenous ecological knowledge for climate resilience
description: Efforts to capture, structure, and preserve indigenous and community-held environmental knowledge in digital form to support conservation and adaptation to climate change. This domain raises questions of ethnographic design, data ownership and marginalization, and the sociotechnical challenge of translating oral/situated knowledge into digital systems without eroding its meaning.
